scenarios.opening_query: Holiday schedules with my ex fall apart every year and this year I want it settled for good.
scenarios.hidden_fact: We actually agree on the fix — alternating holidays in writing; we just need coaching to file the stipulated modification ourselves without paying two retainers.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: We agree on alternating holidays — we just want coaching to file the stipulated modification ourselves.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether an agreed modification needs only filing help
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: We agree on nothing — she calendars over my time deliberately, and a contested modification with a parenting-time enforcement motion is what's needed.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: We agree on nothing — she calendars over my time deliberately. It's a contested modification.
scenarios.vetting_note: Agreement-status selects coaching vs contested work.

scenarios.opening_query: My daughter's father hasn't seen her in three years and now suddenly demands weekends.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Family Law > Child Custody/Visitation || Family Law > Child Support/Modification
scenarios.hidden_fact: His 'demand' arrived the same week as my support-modification filing — his lawyer's letter even offers to drop the weekend demand if I drop the increase; it's a support-leverage play to answer inside the support case.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: His demand arrived the week of my support-modification filing — his lawyer even offered to trade it away. It's support leverage.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the visitation demand is leverage in a support fight
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: No support angle — he's genuinely back, sober two years with documentation, and the parenting-plan question is real.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: No money angle — he's genuinely back and sober two years. The parenting question is real.
scenarios.vetting_note: Leverage-vs-genuine-return; the linkage letter is the fact.

scenarios.opening_query: My son came home from his dad's with bruises and a story that keeps changing.
scenarios.hidden_fact: His pediatrician made the mandatory report yesterday — DHS is assessing both homes now, and everything runs through that assessment first.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The pediatrician made a mandatory report — DHS is assessing both homes, and everything runs through that first.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a child-welfare assessment is open
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The doctor found it consistent with the skate-park fall in the video his dad sent — no report; what's left is our chronic communication failure, and I want the plan's exchange terms fixed.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: The doctor matched it to the skate-park fall on video — no report. What's left is fixing our communication terms.
scenarios.vetting_note: Report-vs-cleared postures handled without minimizing either.

scenarios.opening_query: I want my baby's father involved but his mother keeps inserting herself into every decision.
scenarios.hidden_fact: She just filed her own petition for grandparent visitation and 'decision-making participation' — I was served Tuesday; defending against the third-party petition is the immediate case.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: She filed her own petition for grandparent visitation and decision rights — I was served Tuesday.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a third-party petition is filed
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: Nothing's filed — the fix is a parenting plan with dad that sets boundaries he'll actually hold with her.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: Nothing filed — we need a parenting plan with boundaries dad will hold.

scenarios.opening_query: My twins' other parent moved them into a school district an hour away without asking me.
scenarios.final: Administrative Law > Schools — Special Needs Education
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Family Law > Child Custody/Visitation || Administrative Law > Schools — Special Needs Education
scenarios.hidden_fact: The urgent damage is educational — both boys' IEPs died in the transfer and the new district refuses comparable services; the IEP enforcement can't wait for the custody fight.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The urgent damage is their IEPs — the new district refuses comparable services, and that enforcement can't wait for custody.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether IEP enforcement is the time-critical need
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: School services transferred fine — the unilateral move itself violates our joint-custody terms, and that's the case.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: Services transferred fine — the unilateral move violates our joint custody. That's the case.

scenarios.opening_query: I'm terrified my ex will hurt the kids and me if I push for the support he owes.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Family Law > Restraining Orders || Family Law > Child Support/Modification
scenarios.hidden_fact: The fear is old scars, not new conduct — he's been nonviolent for years and lives out of state; what I actually need is the state's support-enforcement tools that don't require me to confront him at all.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The fear is old scars — no new conduct in years and he's out of state. What I need are enforcement tools that don't involve facing him.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether current conduct supports protection or enforcement can proceed at arm's length
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The threat is current — 'push the support and you'll regret it' by voicemail last week; I want protection in place before any money case.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's current — a 'you'll regret it' voicemail last week. Protection first, money after.
scenarios.vetting_note: Current-conduct fact ordered carefully; neither path minimizes the fear.
